subject: history
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: Ancient trade routes moved goods, ideas, and cultures across long distances.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: How did silk from faraway lands reach distant markets long before aeroplanes? Trade routes connected people.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Follow Source → Route → Market. The diagram shows movement of goods and the exchange of ideas along the way.

sections.title: Concept: Trade Route
sections.body: A trade route is a path merchants used to carry goods. Along it, languages, skills, and beliefs also travelled.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: Trade routes were ancient highways for products and for new ideas.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: Spices valued in one region could be rare elsewhere, so merchants risked long journeys for high reward.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Map a caravan moving cloth east and spices west: each stop may add taxes, rest, and cultural exchange.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Route and explain one challenge travellers might face.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Trade routes carried goods and ideas. Understanding the path helps explain how cultures influenced each other.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: What modern route — road, rail, or shipping — reminds you of ancient trade networks?

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Create a postcard from a merchant describing one goods exchange along the route.

diagram_package.title: Ancient Trade Routes
diagram_package.caption: Source → Route → Market
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Ancient Trade Routes connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Market and explain what arrives there besides goods.
